pi_items.source: setup_task.sh writes requirements to this path.
setup_task.sh document defines GSD as 4.0 cm/pixel.
setup_task.sh document defines camera sensor width as 23.5 mm.
setup_task.sh document defines camera sensor height as 15.6 mm.
setup_task.sh document defines camera image width as 6000 pixels.
setup_task.sh document defines focal length as 16 mm.
setup_task.sh document calculates and explicitly requires target flight altitude of 163.4 m AGL. Confirmed via mathematical check: (0.04 * 16 * 6000) / 23.5 = 163.404.
setup_task.sh document explicitly requires frontal (forward) overlap of 75%.
setup_task.sh document explicitly requires side (lateral) overlap of 65%.

privileged_info_summary: I have audited the QGroundControl survey mission planning task. The task generates a synthetic requirements document dynamically in setup_task.sh. I confirmed that the explicit requirements match the metadata perfectly: the survey requires a GSD of 0.04 m (4.0 cm) using a 16 mm focal length camera with a 23.5 mm sensor and 6000 px image width, which correctly computes to the stated 163.4 m target altitude. Furthermore, the required 75% frontal overlap, 65% side overlap, and expected file paths (/home/ga/Documents/QGC/field_survey.plan and /home/ga/Documents/QGC/survey_requirements.txt) are all completely validated by setup_task.sh and export_result.sh.
